__fixture_tag__: VENDORED UNIVERSAL-FF PREDICTION — frozen NNP output, not measurement
target_property: subharmonic-response peak height above noise floor + spin-coherence time for a driven-Floquet discrete time crystal in a dipolar spin-bath host (NV-center-in-diamond or trapped-ion chain) at 5 K — proxy for the rigidity criterion that distinguishes a genuine discrete time crystal (DTC) from a trivially-periodic Floquet response
predicted_unit: × (subharmonic peak height in units of noise floor) at drive frequency ω₀ / 2

operating_conditions.host: NV-center-in-diamond ensemble (Choi 2017 Nature 543:221) OR ¹⁷¹Yb⁺ trapped-ion chain (Zhang 2017 Nature 543:217)
operating_conditions.drive_protocol: Floquet binary echo sequence — periodic π-pulse train with disorder-induced rigidity window
operating_conditions.subharmonic_signal: Fourier peak at 0.5 ω₀ (period-doubled response to ω₀ drive)
operating_conditions.noise_floor_reference: ensemble dephasing baseline outside the rigidity window
operating_conditions.MBL_window: disorder strength W/J ~ 2-5 to access many-body-localized phase

model_caveat: Universal FFs predict atomistic energies / forces on the host-crystal lattice (diamond + NV defect or Yb-trap chain) — they do NOT directly compute Floquet-eigenstate dynamics or subharmonic response (which is a many-body driven-dissipative open-system property requiring MBL theory + spin-bath master equation). The 15× peak + 50 ms coherence values here are a PROXY-from-literature pinned with raw#10 C3 honesty: Choi 2017 NV-ensemble + Zhang 2017 trapped-ion reports of period-doubled response under disorder-stabilized rigidity. SEMI-QUANTITATIVE — NNP frames host-lattice geometry only.
proxy_source: Yao et al. 2017 PRL 118:030401 (theoretical discrete time crystal in disordered dipolar systems, MBL-stabilized rigidity criterion, original Floquet-DTC framework); Choi et al. 2017 Nature 543:221 (NV-ensemble in diamond, period-doubled response under random π-pulse drive, ~ 100 NV interaction time-scales coherence); Zhang et al. 2017 Nature 543:217 (¹⁷¹Yb⁺ trapped-ion chain DTC, 10-ion programmable quantum simulator); Mi et al. 2022 Nature 601:531 (Sycamore superconducting-qubit DTC, 20-qubit Floquet circuit); Else et al. 2020 Annu. Rev. Condens. Matter Phys. 11:467 (Floquet DTC + MBL review); Khemani et al. 2016 PRL 116:250401 (theoretical rigidity criterion + DTC vs trivial-Floquet distinction)
falsifier_text: subharmonic peak < 10× noise OR coherence < 100 ms → FAIL
unproven_markers_preserved: trivial-time-crystal vs many-body-localized (MBL) distinction HARD_WALL — a periodic 2T-response is necessary but not sufficient for a genuine DTC; rigorous discrimination requires either MBL-eigenstate spectroscopy or proof-of-rigidity (Khemani 2016 / Else 2016) UNVERIFIED at lab scale; preserved verbatim from §4.F.12 risk-flags
Mi 2022 Sycamore superconducting-qubit DTC widely cited but Floquet-trivial reading remains academically contested (Howell 2023 + community DTC re-analysis papers)

coherence ≥ 100 ms target AT-RISK — best-reported NV-ensemble Floquet coherence ~ 50 ms (Choi 2017 + follow-ups); trapped-ion chains and Sycamore reach only 0.1-1 ms in Floquet-eigenstate stabilized rigidity windows
MBL-protected DTC requires disorder strength W/J ~ 2-5 and finite-size scaling UNVERIFIED at thermodynamic limit (Khemani 2016 + Else 2020)
device-layer integration → hexa-chip per CROSS_LINK §3.2 (NV-center qubit array fabrication is sister-substrate domain)
no claim of RT-DTC or ambient operation (5 K cryogenic host)
honesty_caveats: AT-RISK on coherence axis — proxy literature reports ~ 50 ms coherence at 5 K, below the 100 ms F-TC-1 target. Subharmonic-peak axis PASSES proxy (15× above the 10× floor). Falsifier_relation = AT-RISK because the coherence arm is the binding constraint; trivial-time-crystal vs MBL distinction HARD_WALL preserved verbatim — PASS at the subharmonic axis does NOT close the candidate. SPEC_FIRST preserved — does NOT promote to EXTERNAL-VERIFIED. Choi 2017 Nature 543 + Zhang 2017 Nature 543 + Mi 2022 Nature 601 lab anchors all carry the rigidity-vs-trivial-Floquet caveat themselves.
license_note: Peer-reviewed paper values per Nature + APS publisher terms; Lukin (Harvard) + Monroe (Maryland/Duke) + Google Quantum AI Sycamore lab authority; Khemani / Else theoretical-rigidity framework (raw#10 C3, NOT n=6 lattice-fit).
